title: Divide using place value
description: Decompose 3- and 4-digit dividends to divide them by a 1-digit divisor.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equations to solve $972 \div9$.**

||
- | -
$\phantom{=}\greenD{972}\div{\blueD9}$|
$=(~$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]${}\div\blueD9~)+(\greenD{72}\div\blueD9)$ | 
$= ~100~ +~ 8$   |
$=  {}$ [[☃ numeric-input 3]]  |
hints:
Let's split $\greenD{972}$ into two numbers that add to make $\greenD{972}$.

$\greenD{972}\div\blueD9 = (\greenD{900}\div\blueD9)+(\greenD{72}\div\blueD9)$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equations to solve $1{,}248\div4$.**

||
- | -
$\phantom{=}\greenD{1{,}248}\div{\blueD4}$|
$=(\greenD{1{,}200}\div\blueD4~)+(\greenD{40}\div\blueD4) + (\greenD8~\div~\blueD4)$ | 
$=$ [[☃ numeric-input 3]]  $ + ~10~ + ~2$  |
$=$ [[☃ numeric-input 4]]  |
hints:
Let's split $\greenD{1{,}248}$ into three numbers that add to make $\greenD{1{,}248}$.

$\greenD{1{,}248}\div\blueD4 = (\greenD{1{,}200}\div\blueD{4}) + (\greenD{40}\div\blueD{4}) + (\greenD 8\div\blueD{4})$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equations to solve $4{,}563 \div9$.**

||
- | -
$\phantom{=}\greenD{4{,}563}\div{\blueD9}$|
$=(\greenD{4{,}500}\div{}~\blueD9  ) \, + \,(\greenD{63}\div{}$[[☃ numeric-input 2]] $  )$ |
$= 500 + 7$ |
$=  $ [[☃ numeric-input 4]]  |
hints:
Let's split $\greenD{4{,}563}$ into two numbers that add to make $\greenD{4{,}563}$. The divisor stays the same for each.

$\greenD{4{,}563}\div\blueD9=(\greenD{4{,}500}\div {\blueD9}  ) + (\greenD{63}\div{\blueD9} )$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equations to solve $7{,}007 \div7$.**

||
- | -
$\phantom{=}\greenD{7{,}007}\div\blueD7$ |
$=(\greenD{7{,}000}\div~$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$ ~)  + (\greenD{7}\div{} \blueD7 )$ |
$=1{,}000 + \ 1$ |
$=$ [[☃ numeric-input 4]] |
hints:
Let's split $\greenD{7{,}007}$ into two numbers that add to make $\greenD{7{,}007}$. The divisor stays the same for each.

$\greenD{7{,}007}\div\blueD7=(\greenD{7{,}000}\div {\blueD7}  )  + (\greenD{7}\div{\blueD7} )$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equations to solve $1{,}860 \div6$.**

||
- | -
$\phantom{=}\greenD{1{,}860}\div{\blueD6}$ |
$=(\greenD{1{,}800}\div\blueD6  ) \, + \,(\greenD{60}\div\,\blueD6 )$ |
$= 300 ~+~$[[☃ numeric-input 3]]|
$=  $ [[☃ numeric-input 4]]  |
hints:
Let's split $\greenD{1{,}860}$ into two numbers that add to make $\greenD{1{,}860}$. The divisor stays the same for each.

$\greenD{1{,}860}\div\blueD6=(\greenD{1{,}800}\div {\blueD6}  ) + (\greenD{60}\div{\blueD6} )$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equations to solve $4{,}568\div 8$.**

||
- | -
$\phantom{=}\greenD{4{,}568}\div{\blueD 8}$|
$=(\greenD{4{,}000}\div\blueD{8}) + (\greenD{560} {}\div~\blueD{8})+(\greenD{8}\div\blueD{8})$ | 
$= 500~+~$ [[☃ numeric-input 3]]  $+~1$  |
$=$ [[☃ numeric-input 4]]  |
hints:
Let's split $\greenD{4{,}568}$ into three numbers that add to make $\greenD{4{,}568}$.

$\greenD{4{,}568}\div\blueD{8} = (\greenD{4{,}000}\div\blueD{8}) + (\greenD{560}\div\blueD{8}) + (\greenD 8\div\blueD{8})$

assessment_items.question_content:
Nora tried to evaluate an expression step by step, but she made a mistake.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}1{,}539 \div 3\\\\
&=(1{,}500 \div 3) + (9 \div3)&\redE{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
&=500  + 3&\goldE{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
&=503&\purpleE{\text{Step } 3}
\end{align}$

**Find Nora's first mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Nora had a lot of useful mathematical thinking in her work.

She tried to break $1{,}539$ into parts, divide the parts, and then combine the quotients.

Let's see where Nora changed the value of the expression.

###$\redE{\text{Step }1}$

$\begin{align}
\maroonE{1{,}539}\div\blueE3  &= (\maroonE{1{,}500}\div {\blueE3}  )  + (\maroonE{30}\div{\blueE3} ) + (\maroonE{9}\div{\blueE3} )\\\\
&\neq  (\maroonE{1{,}500}\div {\blueE3}  )  +  (\maroonE{9}\div{\blueE3} )
\end{align}$ 

Nora broke apart $1{,}539$ into $1{,}500 + 9$ instead of $1{,}500 + 30 + 9$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Mel tried to evaluate an expression step by step, but they made a mistake.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}6{,}060 \div 5\\\\
&=(6{,}060 \div 3) + (6{,}060 \div2)&\redE{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
&=2{,}020 + 3{,}030&\goldE{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
&=5{,}050&\purpleD{\text{Step } 3}
\end{align}$

**Find Mel's first mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Mel had a lot of useful mathematical thinking in their work.

They tried to break $6{,}060$ into parts, divide the parts, and then combine the quotients.

Let's see where Mel changed the value of the expression.

###$\redE{\text{Step }1}$

$\maroonE{6{,}060} \div \blueE{5} =(\maroonE{6{,}000} \div \blueE{5}) + (\maroonE{60} \div \blueE{5})$

Mel said it equaled $(\redE{6{,}060} \div \redE3) + (\redE{6{,}060} \div\redE2)$, so Mel made a mistake in step $1$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Nate tried to evaluate an expression step by step, but he made a mistake.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}6{,}432 \div 8\\\\
&=(6{,}400 \div 8) - (32 \div8)&\redE{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
&=800 - 4&\goldE{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
&=796&\purpleE{\text{Step } 3}
\end{align}$

**Find Nate's first mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Nate had a lot of useful mathematical thinking in his work.

He tried to break $6{,}432$ into parts, divide the parts, and then subtract the quotients.

Let's see where Nate changed the value of the expression.

###$\redE{\text{Step }1}$

$\maroonE{6{,}432}\div\blueE8  = (\maroonE{6{,}400}\div {\blueE8}  )  + (\maroonE{32}\div{\blueE8} )$

Nate said it equaled $ (\maroonE{6{,}400}\div {\blueE8}  )  \redE- (\maroonE{32}\div{\blueE8} )$, so Nate made a mistake in step $1$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Fiona tried to evaluate an expression step by step, but she made a mistake.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}846 \div 2\\\\
&=(800 \div 2) + (40 \div2) + (6 \div2)&\redE{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
&=800 + 20 + 3&\goldE{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
&=823&\purpleE{\text{Step } 3}
\end{align}$

**Find Fiona's first mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Fiona had a lot of useful mathematical thinking in her work.

She tried to break $846$ into parts, divide the parts, and then combine the quotients.

Let's see where Fiona changed the value of the expression.

###$\redE{\text{Step }1}$

$\greenD{846}\div\blueD2   \stackrel{\checkmark}{=}(\greenD{800}\div {\blueD2}  ) + (\greenD{40}\div{\blueD2} ) + (\greenD{6}\div{\blueD2})$

Fiona did not make a mistake in step $1$.

###$\goldE{\text{Step }2}$

$(\greenD{800}\div {\blueD2}  ) + (\greenD{40}\div{\blueD2} ) + (\greenD{6}\div{\blueD2}) =\ 400 + 20 + 3$ 

Fiona said it equaled $\redD{800} + 20 + 3$, so Fiona made a mistake in step $2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ryan tried to evaluate an expression step by step, but he made a mistake.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}2{,}350 \div 5\\\\
&=(2{,}000 \div 5) + (300 \div5) + (50 \div5)&\redE{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
&=400 + 60 + 10&\goldE{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
&=461&\purpleE{\text{Step } 3}
\end{align}$

**Find Ryan's first mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Ryan had a lot of useful mathematical thinking in his work.

He tried to break $2{,}350$ into parts, divide the parts, and then combine the quotients.

Let's see where Ryan changed the value of the expression.

###$\redE{\text{Step }1}$

$\greenD{2{,}350}\div\blueD5  \stackrel{\checkmark}{=}(\greenD{2{,}000}\div {\blueD5}  ) +(\greenD{300}\div{\blueD5} ) + (\greenD{50}\div{\blueD5} )$ 

Ryan did not make a mistake in step $1$.

###$\goldE{\text{Step }2}$

$(\greenD{2{,}000}\div {\blueD5}  ) +(\greenD{300}\div{\blueD5} ) + (\greenD{50}\div{\blueD5} )\stackrel{\checkmark}{=} 400 + 60 + 10$

Ryan did not make a mistake in step $2$.

###$\purpleE{\text{Step }3}$

$400 + 60 + 10 = 470$ 

Ryan said it equaled $\redD{461}$, so Ryan made a mistake in step $3$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kevin tried to evaluate an expression step by step, but he made a mistake.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}2{,}440 \div 8\\\\
&=(2{,}400 \div 8) + (40 \div8) &\redE{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
&=300 + 5&\goldE{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
&=350&\purpleD{\text{Step } 3}
\end{align}$

**Find Kevin's first mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Kevin had a lot of useful mathematical thinking in his work.

He tried to break $2{,}440$ into parts, divide the parts, and then combine the quotients.

Let's see where Kevin changed the value of the expression.

###$\redE{\text{Step }1}$

$\greenD{2{,}440}\div\blueD8  \stackrel{\checkmark}{=}(\greenD{2{,}400}\div {\blueD8}  )  + (\greenD{40}\div{\blueD8} )$

Kevin did not make a mistake in step $1$.

###$\goldE{\text{Step }2}$

$(\greenD{2{,}400}\div {\blueD8}  )  + (\greenD{40}\div{\blueD8} )\stackrel{\checkmark}{=}300 + 5$

Kevin did not make a mistake in step $2$.

###$\purpleD{\text{Step }3}$

$300 + 5={305}$

Kevin said it equaled $\redD{350}$, so Kevin made a mistake in step $3$.
